mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed negative returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during easing periods or produces a negative Rank IC over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3.0 percent YoY and FEDFUNDS > 2.5 percent
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for yield curve slope.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative returns in more than 50 percent of observations or fails to achieve a positive out-of-sample R-squared in predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, prompting institutional deleveraging that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered instances or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in unemployment confirm a synchronized economic upturn, boosting equity risk appetite and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns in more than 50 percent of cases or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
